indicator: Maltreatment in care (victimizations / 100,000 days in care)
description: Number of victimizations per 100,000 days of care among children in foster care during a 12-month period.
denominator: Total number of days in foster care for children who were in care during the 12-month period (as of the end of the period).
numerator: Number of substantiated or indicated maltreatment reports (by any perpetrator) occurring during a foster care episode within the 12-month period.
risk_adjustment: State; age at entry (for children entering) or age on first day of the 12-month period (for children already in care)
exclusions: Complete episodes lasting <8 days (neither days nor maltreatment counted); maltreatment reported within the first 7 days of removal; reports with incident date outside the foster care episode (when incident date exists); reports with report date outside the 12-month period; subsequent maltreatment reports occurring within 1 day of the initial report.
notes: Cases matched across AFCARS and NCANDS using AFCARS ID; report date determines inclusion in the 12-month window; incident dates used (when present) to check if victimization occurred outside the episode; special handling of prior discharge date to calculate length of stay when missing in first file; two years of NCANDS used to ensure the report falls in the 12-month period.
direction_legend: A lower value is more desirable.

description: Percent of children with a substantiated or indicated maltreatment report during a 12-month period who experience another substantiated or indicated report within 12 months of the initial report.
denominator: Number of children with at least one substantiated or indicated maltreatment report during a 12-month period.
numerator: Number of children in the denominator who experience another substantiated or indicated maltreatment report within 12 months of the initial report.
exclusions: Subsequent victimizations with a report date within 14 days of the initial report; subsequent reports where the incident date matches the initial incident (treated as the same incident); unborn children.
notes: Report date is used to determine whether the initial victimization occurred during the 12-month period. Incident dates are used only to detect duplicates that refer to the same incident.
direction_legend: A lower value is more desirable.

description: Number of children who enter foster care per 1,000 children in the child population during a 12-month period.
denominator: Number of children in the child population (per census).
numerator: Number of children who enter foster care in a 12-month period.
direction_legend: A lower value is more desirable.

indicator: Permanency in 12 months for children entering care
description: Percent of children who enter foster care in a 12-month period who discharge to permanency within 12 months of entering care.
denominator: Number of children who enter foster care during a 12-month period.
numerator: Number of children in the denominator who discharge to permanency within 12 months of entering care.
risk_adjustment: State; age at entry; state's foster care entry rate
exclusions: Children in foster care <8 days; children without a discharge reason; children who reenter in the same reporting period due to reporting structure not including discharge reason for the first episode.
notes: Trial home visit (THV) adjustment: any THV time exceeding 30 days is discounted when the child discharges to reunification after a THV placement setting.
direction_legend: A higher value is more desirable.

indicator: Permanency in 12 months for children in care 12-23 months
description: Percent of children in foster care on the first day of a 12-month period who had been in care continuously for 12-23 months and who discharge to permanency within 12 months of the first day.
denominator: Number of children in foster care on the first day of the 12-month period who had been in care continuously for 12-23 months.
numerator: Number of children in the denominator who discharge to permanency within 12 months of the first day of the 12-month period.
exclusions: Children without a discharge reason; children who reenter foster care in the same reporting period due to reporting structure not including discharge reason for the first episode.
direction_legend: A higher value is more desirable.

indicator: Permanency in 12 months for children in care 24 months or more
indicator_short: Perm in 12 months (24 months or more)
description: Percent of children in foster care on the first day of a 12-month period who had been in care continuously for 24 months or more and who discharge to permanency within 12 months of the first day.
denominator: Number of children in foster care on the first day of the 12-month period who had been in care continuously for 24 months or more.
numerator: Number of children in the denominator who discharge to permanency within 12 months of the first day of the 12-month period.
exclusions: Children without a discharge reason; children who re‑enter foster care in the same reporting period due to reporting structure not including discharge reason for the first episode.
direction_legend: A higher value is more desirable.

indicator: Reentry to foster care within 12 months
indicator_short: Reentry to foster care within 12 months
description: Percent of children who exit foster care in a 12-month period to permanency and who reenter foster care within 12 months of discharge.
denominator: Number of children who discharge from foster care during a 12-month period to permanency.
numerator: Number of children in the denominator who reenter foster care within 12 months of their discharge to permanency.
exclusions: Children whose first episode discharge lacks a discharge reason; records where the reentry removal date is before the prior discharge date; children who reenter in the same reporting period due to reporting structure not including discharge reason for the first episode.
notes: If a child has multiple reentries within 12 months, only the first reentry is counted.
direction_legend: A lower value is more desirable.

description: Number of placement moves per 1,000 days of foster care among children who enter care during a 12-month period.
denominator: Total number of days in foster care for children who enter care in the 12-month period (as of the end of the period).
numerator: For children who enter care during the 12-month period, the number of placement moves during that period (initial removal into foster care is not counted as a move).
exclusions: Children in foster care <8 days; days in care where placement information cannot be determined.
notes: When discharge date is missing in the first 6-month file but present as 'date of prior discharge' in the next file, that date is used to calculate length of stay.
direction_legend: A lower value is more desirable.
